item.title: Australian technology companies making it big on the world stage
item.description: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

item.description: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto...

item.description: Here's what you need to know about Mario Tennis Fever, Pragmata and Resident Evil Requiem.

item.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
item.description: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Apple's latest iPhone is designed for those clinging onto older phones
item.description: A lot of people using older model phones are contemplating an upgrade.

item.title: Bigfoot, trombone, orca among the latest emojis
item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.description: That's AUD, not USD. How does the laptop market survive in its current form when the MacBook Neo exists?

item.description: T﻿ech company bosses have had similar things to say about AI.

item.title: Aussies heading to the USA set for high-speed Wi-Fi in a travel revolution
item.description: Australians heading to the United States are set for a big change in the way we travel, with an all-new in-flight experience on the next-generation of aircraft from this airline.

item.title: All households to suffer if 'central' industry buckles under soaring fuel costs
item.description: The global fuel crisis has left one of the nation's most essential sectors heavily exposed.

item.title: Mining giant handed $2 billion in taxpayer funds to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running
item.description: Rio Tinto has secured a $2 billion taxpayer handout to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running in a bid to save 3000 jobs.

item.title: Sydney Opera House sails light up to mark 50 Years of Apple
item.description: The sails of the Sydney Opera House will light up with colour over the next three nights as part of a global celebration of Apple's 50th birthday.

item.title: 'Almost everything has gone up': Payday super laws are coming at a fraught time for small business owners
item.description: Rebecca Foley, who co-owns Sprout Artisan Bakery in Brisbane, said ﻿unpredictable costs and new compliances have been creeping into her profit margin for years.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands addresses media after exiting court in $100 million showdown with ARN
item.description: The top-rating provocateur is suing his former employer ARN in the Federal Court.

item.title: Martina turned a childhood pastime into a seven-figure career
item.description: It was all thanks to a little nudge from her mother on an international family trip when she was eight. ﻿

item.description: The figures don't paint an overly pretty picture for Australia – although the government says there's a good reason for it.

item.description: "That will help control the costs in the supply chain."

item.description: H﻿ow the company came back from the brink to become a global juggernaut.

item.title: Australia to phase out discounted junior wages for young workers

item.title: Australian cosmetics giant Mecca handed $594,000 fine for failing to lodge audited financial reports on time
item.description: Mecca's parent company and associated businesses have each coughed up ﻿$198,000 in fines after being accused of late lodgements by the corporate watchdog.

item.description: T﻿he Reserve Bank said the fees were no longer influencing people to make more efficient payments.

item.description: The store will continue trading until October 2026.

item.description: "The more I looked at it, the more I discovered red flags."

item.description: T﻿he CEO admitted "the roles people play are shifting".

item.title: Virgin to cut flights, raise costs after $40 million fuel hit
item.description: Virgin Australia has joined Qantas in cutting flights and hiking ticket prices as skyrocketing fuel prices hit local airlines.

item.description: F﻿ollowing years of rumours, Apple may be about to deliver a missing link in its iPhone range.

item.description: A﻿ nation-wide study of our wastewater and online search data has revealed the worrying trend is booming.

item.description: Cook, who took over from the late Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain involved with Apple.

item.description: Health Minister Mark Butler delivered the sweeping changes in a National Press Club Address and said the NDIS will no longer ﻿"be an ATM for shonks, grifters, fraudsters and crooks".

item.description: The government is set to slash the cost of running the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) and aged care under sweeping new changes.

item.description: T﻿he inquiry has been marked by scuffles between investigators and delivery service employees, a feigned medical emergency and hastily scribbled notes to "stay silent".

item.description: Many critics have sounded the alarm on joining two of Hollywood's biggest remaining five studios.

item.description: People used to shame it, but clearly something is working for Aussie shoppers.

item.description: Woolworths has seen a jump in grocery sales but doesn't expect it to last, slashing its profit forecasts amid high petrol prices.

item.description: The Reserve Bank's third successive rate hike hit mortgage holders like a kick in the guts and there is little light to be found at the end of the tunnel when digging deeper.

item.description: Donald Trump wants the Supreme Court to halt a $A115 million payout he owes to E Jean Carroll, a woman a New York jury found he raped in the mid-1990s.

item.description: The $20,000 threshold will apply per asset and will expand across tools, technology or other assets, both new and second-hand, and can be claimed in full at the end of every financial year.﻿

item.description: Anthony Albanese is being mock-hailed as a 47 per cent "partner" by business owners online in a wave of AI memes.

item.description: Last week's changes to the ﻿capital gains tax discount and negative gearing will likely shift people away from rentvesting.

item.description: The ex-partner of former Labor leader Mark Latham might have dropped her application for court protection but some grievances remain in the public domain.

item.description: A controversial weapons exposition will return to Melbourne in two years' time, despite the last event sparking some of the city's largest protests in decades.

item.title: 'They've got a real China problem': US senators question Trump's pick for ambassador to Australia
item.description: US President Donald Trump's pick for ambassador to Australia has pledged to focus on AUKUS, critical minerals, antisemitism and China's growing influence in the region.

item.description: T﻿he former prime minister has criticised wealthy people's "howls" about the current government's tax reform. ﻿

item.title: 'Won't be some socialist takeover': Pauline Hanson unveils gas policy
item.description: T﻿he One Nation leader would upend the oil and gas sector by scrapping a popular tax and bringing the government in as a 30 per cent partner under her gas policy.

item.description: T﻿here have been more than 230 cases in most states and territories.

item.description: The number of unemployed Aussies has soared by 33,000 in the past month.

item.description: The man in charge of bringing Brisbane 2032 to life is facing the prospect of a criminal investigation into whether he committed electoral fraud.

item.title: Opposition leader backs Jacinta Price after questions about migration remark
item.description: Jacinta Price denied agreeing to a remark that Australia needs to stop "flooding" itself with migrants from India, China, Africa and the Middle East.

item.title: Government 'unaware' of booked flights for ISIS brides returning home
item.description: Six women, their children and grandchildren have reportedly left a Syrian camp for Australia.

item.title: Guzman y Gomez scuttles plans for US expansion after weak sales, immediately scraps eight stores
item.description: G﻿uzman y Gomez has scuttled its plans for an ambitious expansion in the United States and will immediately shut its existing stores in Chicago.

item.description: T﻿he One Nation leader made the remark just seconds after her adviser said 'shut up' to a journalist.

item.title: PM refuses to rule out changes to the 'death tax'
item.description: Labor has been fielding questions about the so-called death tax during its nationwide campaign to promote the budget, which it has dismissed as a Coalition scare campaign.

item.title: One Nation would form federal opposition, Labor re-elected, according to new polling
item.description: Federal Labor would be returned to power, the Coalition wiped from the map in four states and One Nation the official opposition party.

item.description: The force of the explosion on Sunday caused two of the train cars to overturn and catch fire, sending thick black smoke into the air, according to footage shared online.

item.description: T﻿he video, shot in Austria, captures the moment a small plane tears through a paraglider's canopy.

item.description: A 23-year-old man has been charged with murder after a newborn baby died in hospital following an incident in Perth's north.

item.description: For 12 weeks, Tom Kean hasn't shown up to work.

item.description: About 50 protesters have taken to ﻿Parliament House in Canberra, leading to police and security shutting down the main entrance.

item.description: T﻿he group are believed to have entered the cave seeking gold before a flash flood blocked the exit.

item.title: Four dead, five injured in crash between train and school bus in Belgium
item.description: The train was estimated to be travelling at about 120km/h as it approached the crossing and had "no time to brake".

item.title: 'No relation to reality': Son suspected of killing billionaire dad steps down, protests innocence
item.description: Jonathan Andic, 45, was arrested in Barcelona last week, 17 months after his father, Mango founder Isak Andic, fell more than 100 metres to his death.

item.title: 'Far better off': Australian bosses warn of mass exodus to NZ and USA after sweeping tax changes
item.description: NZ finance minister Nicola Willis may have been tongue-in-cheek when she invited Australians to move across the ditch, but it appears many business owners are seriously considering the move.

item.title: Today in History - May 27: Out-of-control fire under small town expected to burn for centuries
item.description: After years as an underground trash dump, a disused mine shaft in Centralia, Pennsylvania had become a foul-smelling haven for vermin.

item.title: Trump says he's in 'perfect' health after medical check-up
item.description: T﻿he 79-year-old president declared doctors gave him the all-clear despite concerns about his advancing age.

item.title: Teenager with Tourette's barred from flight after he yells out 'bomb'
item.description: An English family's holiday plans were thrown into chaos after British Airways barred a 13-year-old teenager with Tourette syndrome from boarding his flight.

item.description: This week's wet weather is only set to get worse.

item.title: NASA unveils plans to send humans back to the moon before Trump's presidency ends in 2028
item.description: The space agency released details of a three-phase moon mission that will eventually build habitable infrastructure o the moon to allow humans to "live and operate" on the lunar service.

item.title: Trump's handpicked candidate may cost his party their most important state

item.description: The first quintuplets to survive beyond infancy were born in a small Canadian town on May 28, 1934.

item.description: Joe Biden's wife feared he was having a stroke during his disastrous presidential debate with Donald Trump in 2024.

item.title: Notorious paedophile Ashley Paul Griffith fights 'excessive' life sentence
item.description: One of Australia's worst paedophiles, childcare worker Ashley Paul Griffith, has launched a bid to slash the life sentence he received for abusing dozens of young children.

item.description: US and Iranian negotiators reached a tentative agreement to extend the ceasefire in the three-month-old war by 60 days and launch talks on Iran's nuclear program.

item.description: Queen Victoria was unharmed during the second assassination attempt on her life on May 29, 1842.

item.description: A﻿n Austrian court sentenced the 21-year-old man to 15 years in jail over the terror attack plan.

item.description: The US government is working on a $250 note to bear the face of Donald Trump, his treasury secretary has announced.

item.title: Son locked his dementia-suffering father in blacked-out room to steal his fortune
item.description: An elderly New Zealand man with advanced dementia was subjected to what his son later admitted was "torture" inside a locked, blacked-out room while family members systematically stole his life savings.

item.description: T﻿he management of a whistleblower's allegations has ultimately been found to be insufficient.

item.description: A drone carrying more than $1 million of contraband into Townsville Correctional Complex in North Queensland has been intercepted by ﻿authorities.

item.title: Protesters arrested at new Olympic stadium site Victoria Park
item.description: Police have arrested two people at Victoria Park, after council officers approached First Nations protesters who have been camping out in opposition to the construction of the 63,000 seat Brisbane Stadium, the centrepiece of the Brisbane 2032 Olympic Games.

item.title: Aussie state bracing for 'once-in-five-year' storm bringing 'risk of tornadoes'
item.description: Huge swells, destructive winds and heavy rain will accompany "strongest winter system we see this year".

item.title: McDonald's wants to come to this historic Victorian town. Its youngest residents say no
item.description: McDonald's has never set foot in the gold rush town. Some of Castlemaine's youngest residents want to keep it that way.

item.title: Trump still has total control of the GOP - and that could doom them
item.description: O﻿pposing the president gets you nowhere in the Republican Party - but voters are another matter.

item.title: Today in History - May 30: Two men killed by same cricket ball-sized sphere, a year apart
item.description: Canadian nuclear physicist Louis Slotin died in hospital on May 30, 1946, nine days after receiving a blast of hard radiation.

item.title: Aussies forked out more than $4.1 billion on bank fees last financial year
item.description: They're costing us all more than $4.1 billion annually - but they don't have to.

item.title: Trump lashes out after judge stops him renaming Kennedy Centre after himself
item.description: Donald Trump has lashed out at a judge who ruled that arts institute The Kennedy Centre could not be renamed after him.

item.title: Majority of nine.com.au readers agree teen social media ban isn't working
item.description: Opinions are divided nearly six months into the teen social media ban.

item.title: Free tickets to soldiers for Trump's UFC event, but only if they are thin enough
item.description: US service members will be offered the chance to attend the UFC fight at the White House, but only if they meet military body composition standards.

item.title: Experts sound the alarm over sexism in schools as boys target female teachers and students
item.description: Even as many parents insist "my boy wouldn't do that".

item.title: Vivid drone show scrapped for good after 'technical issue' sent 89 plummeting into Sydney harbour
item.description: 'Unforeseen technical issues' have forced the festival to cancel the popular performance.

item.title: US commander meets with Cuban military officials as Trump pressures island nation
item.description: The top US commander in Latin America met with Cuban military leaders on Friday in a “brief exchange on operational security matters” near the US Navy base in Guantanamo Bay, the latest official to visit the island nation as President Donald Trump ramps up pressure on its leaders.

item.title: Trump's doctor recommends he lose weight and exercise more but says he is in 'excellent health'
item.description: The White House released the results of President Donald Trump's May physical late on Friday evening (local time), sharing a memo from his physician recommending he lose weight and exercise more while noting he is in excellent health.

item.title: Thieves ram ute into shopping centre, steal ATM in wild CCTV footage
item.description: T﻿he incident occurred in the dead of night at a shopping centre in WA .

item.title: Professor Richard Scolyer's wife shares sad update in his brain cancer battle
item.description: The acclaimed m﻿elanoma expert and former Australian of the Year was diagnosed with a grade four glioblastoma in 2023.

item.title: Leaked diphtheria data shows cases surged weeks before public health alert in Northern Territory
item.description: An NT Health spokesperson said the department acted appropriately.﻿

item.title: Resident found dead, crime scene declared after unit fire on the Gold Coast
item.description: A person has been found dead and a crime scene has been declared after a unit fire on the Gold Coast.

item.title: Liberals leader fires up party faithful following Tony Abbott's presidency
item.description: Opposition leader issues rallying cry on the final day of the federal Liberal Council in Melbourne.

item.title: Man beaten and left for dead after asking partiers to turn down music in Sydney
item.description: A Sydney man has been beaten up and left for dead in a park near his home - after telling a group of partiers to turn the music down.

item.title: Man hit twice by car during alleged wild brawl on Melbourne footpath caught on CCTV
item.description: Two men have allegedly attacked each other with metal poles during a wild street brawl in Melbourne's west﻿, captured on CCTV.

item.title: Rescuers free four men who had been trapped in a flooded Laos cave, search for two still missing
item.description: Rescue workers in Laos have safely evacuated four remaining villagers trapped in a flooded cave for 10 days, a day after another one was successfully extracted.

item.title: Retiring police officer the target of man's car, court hears
item.description: A man accused of ploughing his car into Mudgee Police Station allegedly told police that's what he was going to do.

item.title: First home deposit scheme backfires as house prices tumble
item.description: More than 50,000 first home buyers are at risk of losing money after house prices tumbled in some capital cities.

item.title: 'Squeezing my face through mud': Aussie diver on 'pretty full on' operation to free villagers trapped in Laos
item.description: South Australian cave diver Josh Richards joined the international rescue effort and told 9News some conditions were more intense than he anticipated.

item.title: Today in History - May 31: Conman exposed by what he ordered from the menu
item.description: A long-time fraudster posing as a Saudi prince was jailed for 18 years on May 31, 2019.

item.title: Man charged after wild brawl at Sydney soccer match
item.description: Police were told a number of spectators entered the field after a fight broke between players in the match.

item.title: Australia's goat meat exports smash records amid surging global demand
item.description: Record production and booming overseas demand have propelled Australia's goat meat industry to new heights.

item.title: Donald Trump to headline 'Great American State Fair' festival after artists drop out
item.description: T﻿rump claimed some artists had "got the yips", as he decried their withdrawals.

item.title: British couple Victoria and Damien moved to Perth to chase the 'Aussie dream'. They flew home within seven mon
item.description: When the Byrons touched down in Perth in 2022, they made one big mistake.

item.title: WHO chief visits epicentre of the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo as cases outpace response
item.description: T﻿he country is experiencing an outbreak of a rare strain of the virus that has no vaccine or cure.

item.title: Israeli army captures strategic castle in Lebanon in deepest incursion into country in 26 years
item.description: Israeli troops have captured a strategic mountain topped with a Crusader-built castle in southern Lebanon in their deepest incursion into the country in more than a quarter of a century.

item.title: Man arrested after reports of shots fired at Sydney home
item.description: Police were called to reports of shots being fired in Sydney's south-west.

item.title: One Nation overtakes Labor as Australia's most popular party in new poll
item.description: In a massive political disruption, a new poll reveals One Nation is now the most popular party in the country, leapfrogging Labor in primary support.

item.title: Man accused of stalking Norwegian princess studying in Sydney
item.description: A Sydney man will face court this week, accused of stalking the potential future queen of Norway, while she studies here in Australia.

item.title: 'I was definitely dying': Tradie relives horror Melbourne road rage attack
item.description: A tradie has relived his road rage nightmare after surviving an axe attack following a minor crash.

item.title: Police hunt man who allegedly stabbed his sister and cousin in Melbourne
item.description: A man accused of stabbing his cousin before ramming a car into his sister's Melbourne home and stabbing her remains on the run hours after the alleged violent rampage.

item.title: Building of Brisbane Olympic Stadium at Victoria Park to begin, despite final protests
item.description: Victoria Park will be closed to the public as building finally begins on Brisbane Olympic Stadium.

item.title: Shirtless Aussie questioned over alleged cafe rampage in Vietnam
item.description: He's accused of causing thousands of dollars of damage in the city of Da Nang before being marched away by police in his underwear.

item.title: Murder victim's family lose home in alleged arson attack
item.description: ﻿It's been six years since the Beaumont family had their lives forever changed. Now their slain son's half brother has been accused of trying to kill them.

item.title: 'Distressing' development as human remains found in search of missing man Peter Willoughby
item.description: P﻿eter Willoughby had been missing since October last year.

item.title: Man fled to neighbours before arrest over cannabis plants and other drugs, police say
item.description: A 43-year-old man allegedly tried to flee into a neighbours home as police arrived to search his house.

item.title: Man, 93, killed in collision with garbage truck
item.description: A 93-year old man driving an SUV has died after a collision with a garbage truck.
